hero.subtitle: I build full-stack applications with Java and React, and I'm exploring how AI tools can change the way developers write code.

projects.title: Projects.
projects.subtitle: Each project starts with a real problem. Here's how I approached them.

skills.title: Skills.
skills.subtitle: Technologies I use daily to build full-stack applications.

engineering.title: Engineering.
engineering.subtitle: How this portfolio is built — architecture decisions I can explain in interviews.

items.title: i18n without heavy frameworks
items.description: Built a lightweight internationalization system using React Context + JSON locale files. No external i18n library. Switching languages updates the entire UI from a single data source — easy to maintain, easy to explain.

items.title: SEO with Next.js SSG
items.description: Next.js pre-renders pages as static HTML at build time. Search engines see complete content instead of an empty div. Added Open Graph and Twitter Card meta for social sharing previews.

items.title: Data-driven architecture
items.description: All page content is driven by JSON data files. Components receive data through props — no hardcoded strings in UI code. Adding new content means editing one file, not touching components.

items.title: Performance-conscious choices
items.description: Native lazy loading on images, async decoding, static page generation, minimal client-side JavaScript. The homepage ships under 3KB of page-specific JS.

about.title: About.
about.motivation: I started coding because I wanted to build things that work — not just assignments, but tools that solve real problems. When I discovered Spring Boot could turn my ideas into running APIs in minutes, I was hooked.
about.direction: Right now I'm focused on two things: getting better at system design (how services talk to each other, how data flows, how things scale), and learning to work with AI coding tools like Cursor and Codex. I believe developers who learn to collaborate with AI will build faster and think bigger.
about.mindset: I prefer building over theorizing. Every project in this portfolio is something I actually built, broke, debugged, and shipped. That's how I learn.
